She is given to you today and here ever after. May the Lord of heaven prosper you both tonight, son, and grant you mercy and peace.”
text.NRSV: But let me tell you the truth, my child. I have given her in marriage to seven men, all of them our kinsmen, and all died in the night when they went in to her. But now, my child, eat and drink, and the Lord will act on behalf of you both.” But Tobias said, “I will neither eat nor drink anything until you settle the things that pertain to me.” So Raguel said, “I will do so. She is given to you in accordance with the decree in the book of Moses, and it has been ordained from heaven that she be given to you. Take your kinswoman; from now on you are her brother and she is your sister. She is given to you from today and forever. May the Lord of heaven, my child, guide and prosper you both this night and grant you mercy and peace.”
text.DRB: Which word Raguel hearing, was sore afraid, knowing what had chanced to those seven husbands, which went in unto her: and he began to fear lest perhaps, it might chance to him also in like manner: and when he doubted, and gave no answer to him demanding,

text.KJV: Raguel said, Then take her from hencefoorth according to the manner, for thou art her cousin, and she is thine, and the mercifull God giue you good successe in all things.
text.NABRE: Then Raguel called his daughter Sarah, and she came to him. He took her by the hand and gave her to Tobiah with these words: “Take her according to the law. According to the decree written in the Book of Moses I give her to be your wife. Take her and bring her safely to your father. And may the God of heaven grant both of you a safe journey in peace!”
text.NRSV: Then Raguel summoned his daughter Sarah. When she came to him he took her by the hand and gave her to Tobias, saying, “Take her to be your wife in accordance with the law and decree written in the book of Moses. Take her and bring her safely to your father. And may the God of heaven prosper your journey with his peace.”
text.DRB: the Angel said to him: Fear not to give her to this man, for to him fearing God is thy daughter due to be his wife: therefore another could not have her.

text.KJV: Then he called his daughter Sara, and she came to her father, and hee tooke her by the hand, and gaue her to be wife to Tobias, saying, Behold, take her after the Law of Moses, and leade her away to thy father: And he blessed them,
text.NABRE: He then called her mother and told her to bring writing materials. He wrote out a copy of a marriage contract stating that he gave Sarah to Tobiah as his wife as decreed by the law of Moses. Her mother brought the material, and he drew up the contract, to which he affixed his seal.
text.NRSV: Then he called her mother and told her to bring writing material; and he wrote out a copy of a marriage contract, to the effect that he gave her to him as wife according to the decree of the law of Moses.
text.DRB: Then said Raguel: I doubt not but God hath admitted my prayers and tears in his sight.

text.KJV: And called Edna his wife, & tooke paper, and did write an instrument of couenants, and sealed it.
text.NABRE: Afterward they began to eat and drink.
text.NRSV: Then they began to eat and drink.
text.DRB: And I believe that therefore he hath made you come to me, that this maid might be joined to her kindred, according to the law of Moyses: and now have no doubt but I will deliver her to thee.

text.KJV: Then they began to eate.
text.NABRE: Later Raguel called his wife Edna and said, “My sister, prepare the other bedroom and bring Sarah there.”
text.NRSV: Raguel called his wife Edna and said to her, “Sister, prepare the other room, and take her in there.”
text.DRB: And taking his daughter by the right hand, gave it into the right hand of Tobias, saying: The God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob be with you, and he join you together, and fulfill his blessing in you.

text.KJV: After Raguel called his wife Edna, and said vnto her, Sister, prepare another chamber, & bring her in thither.
text.NABRE: She went, made the bed in the room, as he had told her, and brought Sarah there. After she had cried over her, she wiped away her tears and said,
text.NRSV: So she went and made the bed in that room as he had told her, and began to weep. Then she wiped away her tears and said to her, “Take courage, my daughter; may the Lord of heaven grant you joy in place of your sorrow. Take courage, my daughter.” Then she went out.
text.DRB: And taking paper they made a writing of the marriage.
And after these things they made merry, blessing God.
text.NABRE: “Take courage, my daughter! May the Lord of heaven grant you joy in place of your grief! Courage, my daughter!” Then she left.
text.KJV: Which when she had done as hee had bidden her, she brought her thither, and she wept, & she receiued the teares of her daughter, and said vnto her,

text.DRB: And Raguel called to him Anna his wife, and commanded her to prepare another chamber.
text.KJV: Be of good comfort, my daughter, the Lord of heauen and earth giue thee ioy for this thy sorow: be of good comfort, my daughter.

text.DRB: And she brought Sara her daughter in thither, and she wept.
And she said to her: Be of good cheer my daughter, our Lord of heaven give thee joy for the tediousness which thou has suffered.

text.KJV: And when they had supped, they brought Tobias in vnto her.
text.NABRE: Chapter 8 Expulsion of the Demon. When they had finished eating and drinking, they wanted to retire. So they brought the young man out and led him to the bedroom.
text.NRSV: When they had finished eating and drinking they wanted to retire; so they took the young man and brought him into the bridal chamber.
text.DRB: And after they had supped, they brought in the young man to her.

text.KJV: And as he went, he remembred the wordes of Raphael, and tooke the ashes of the perfumes, and put the heart, and the liuer of the fish thereupon, and made a smoke therewith.
text.NABRE: Tobiah, mindful of Raphael’s instructions, took the fish’s liver and heart from the bag where he had them, and put them on the embers intended for incense.
text.NRSV: Then Tobias remembered the words of Raphael, and he took the fish’s liver and heart out of the bag where he had them and put them on the embers of the incense.
text.DRB: Tobias therefore remembering the Angel's word, brought forth out of his bag part of the liver, & laid it upon live coals.

text.KJV: The which smell, when the euill spirit had smelled, hee fled into the outmost parts of Egypt, and the Angel bound him.
text.NABRE: The odor of the fish repulsed the demon, and it fled to the upper regions of Egypt; Raphael went in pursuit of it and there bound it hand and foot. Then Raphael returned immediately.
text.NRSV: The odor of the fish so repelled the demon that he fled to the remotest parts of Egypt. But Raphael followed him, and at once bound him there hand and foot.
text.DRB: Then Raphael the Angel took the devil, & bound him in the desert of higher Ægypt.

text.KJV: And after that they were both shut in together, Tobias rose out of the bed and said, Sister, arise, and let vs pray, that God would haue pitie on vs.
text.NABRE: When Sarah’s parents left the bedroom and closed the door behind them, Tobiah rose from bed and said to his wife, “My sister, come, let us pray and beg our Lord to grant us mercy and protection.”
text.NRSV: When the parents had gone out and shut the door of the room, Tobias got out of bed and said to Sarah, “Sister, get up; let us pray and implore our Lord that he may grant us mercy and safety.”
text.DRB: Then Tobias exhorted the virgin, & said to her: Sara arise, & let us pray to God today, and tomorrow, and the next morrow: because these three nights we are joined to God: & when the third night is past, we will be in our wedlock.

text.KJV: Then began Tobias to say, Blessed art thou, O God of our fathers, and blessed is thy holy and glorious Name for euer, let the heauens blesse thee, and all thy creatures.
text.NABRE: She got up, and they started to pray and beg that they might be protected. He began with these words: “Blessed are you, O God of our ancestors; blessed be your name forever and ever! Let the heavens and all your creation bless you forever.
text.NRSV: So she got up, and they began to pray and implore that they might be kept safe. Tobias began by saying, “Blessed are you, O God of our ancestors, and blessed is your name in all generations forever. Let the heavens and the whole creation bless you forever.”
text.DRB: For we are the children of holy men, and we may not be joined together as gentiles, that know not God.

text.KJV: Thou madest Adam, and gauest him Eue his wife for an helper & stay: of them came mankind: thou hast said, It is not good that man should bee alone, let vs make vnto him an aide like to himselfe.
text.NABRE: You made Adam, and you made his wife Eve to be his helper and support; and from these two the human race has come. You said, ‘It is not good for the man to be alone; let us make him a helper like himself.’
text.NRSV: “You made Adam, and for him you made his wife Eve as a helper and support. From the two of them the human race has sprung. You said, ‘It is not good that the man should be alone; let us make a helper for him like himself.’”
text.DRB: And they rising together, prayed both together that health might be given them.

text.KJV: And now, O Lord, I take not this my sister for lust, but vprightly: therefore mercifully ordeine, that wee may become aged together.
text.NABRE: Now, not with lust, but with fidelity I take this kinswoman as my wife. Send down your mercy on me and on her, and grant that we may grow old together. Bless us with children.”
text.NRSV: “I now am taking this kinswoman of mine, not because of lust, but with sincerity. Grant that she and I may find mercy and that we may grow old together.”
text.DRB: And Tobias said: Lord God of our fathers, the heavens, and the earth, and the sea, and fountains, and rivers, and all thy creatures that are in them, bless thee.

text.KJV: And she said with him, Amen.
text.NABRE: They said together, “Amen, amen!”
text.NRSV: And they both said, “Amen, Amen.”
text.DRB: Thou madest Adam of the slime of the earth, and gavest him Eve an helper.

text.KJV: So they slept both that night, and Raguel arose, and went & made a graue
text.NABRE: Then they went to bed for the night. But Raguel got up and summoned his servants. They went out with him and dug a grave,
text.NRSV: Then they went to sleep for the night. But Raguel got up and called his servants to him, and they went and dug a grave,
text.DRB: And now Lord thou knowest, that not for fleshly lust do I take my sister to wife, but only for the love of posterity, in the which thy name may be blessed for ever & ever.

text.KJV: Saying, I feare lest he be dead.
text.NABRE: for he said, “Perhaps Tobiah will die; then we would be a laughingstock and an object of mockery.”
text.NRSV: for he said, “It is possible that he will die and we will become an object of ridicule and derision.”
text.DRB: Sara also said. Have mercy on us Lord, have mercy upon us, and let us grow old both together in health.

text.KJV: But when Raguel was come into his house,
text.NABRE: When they had finished digging the grave, Raguel went back into the house and called his wife,
text.NRSV: When they had finished digging the grave, Raguel went into his house and called his wife,
text.DRB: And it came to pass about the cocks' crowing, Raguel bade his servants to be called for, and they went with him together to dig a grave.

text.KJV: He said vnto his wife Edna, Send one of the maids, and let her see, whether he be aliue: if he be not, that we may bury him, and no man know it.
text.NABRE: saying, “Send one of the maids in to see whether he is alive. If he has died, let us bury him without anyone knowing about it.”
text.NRSV: saying, “Send one of the maids and have her go in to see if he is alive. But if he is dead, let us bury him at once, so that no one will know.”
text.DRB: For he said: Lest perhaps it may chance to him, as also to the other seven husbands that went in unto her.

text.KJV: So the maid opened the doore and went in, and found them both asleepe,
text.NABRE: They sent the maid, lit a lamp, and opened the bedroom door; she went in and found them sleeping together.
text.NRSV: So they sent the maid, lit a lamp, and opened the door; and she went in and found them asleep together.
text.DRB: And when they had prepared the pit, Raguel returning to his wife, said to her:

text.KJV: And came forth, and told them, that he was aliue.
text.NABRE: The maid came out and told them that Tobiah was alive, and that nothing was wrong.
text.NRSV: Then the maid came out and informed them that he was alive and that nothing was wrong.
text.DRB: Send one of thy handmaids, & let her see if he be dead, that I may bury him before it be day.

text.KJV: Then Raguel praised God, and said, O God, thou art worthy to be praised with all pure and holy praise: therefore let thy Saints praise thee with all thy creatures, and let all thine Angels and thine elect praise thee for euer.
text.NABRE: Then they praised the God of heaven in these words: “Blessed are you, God, with every pure blessing! Let all your chosen ones bless you forever!
text.NRSV: So they blessed the God of heaven, and Raguel said, “Blessed are you, O God, with every pure blessing; let all your chosen ones bless you. Let them bless you forever.”
text.DRB: But she sent one of her handmaids, who going into the chamber, found them safe & sound, sleeping both together.

text.KJV: Thou art to be praised, for thou hast made mee ioyfull, and that is not come to me, which I suspected: but thou hast dealt with vs according to thy great mercie.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you, for you have made me happy; what I feared did not happen. Rather you have dealt with us according to your abundant mercy.
text.NRSV: “Blessed are you because you have made me glad. It has not happened as I expected; but you have treated us according to your great mercy.”
text.DRB: And returning she brought good tidings: and they blessed our Lord, to wit, Raguel & Anna his wife,

text.KJV: Thou art to be praised, because thou hast had mercie of two, that were the onely begotten children of their fathers, grant them mercy, O Lord, and finish their life in health, with ioy and mercie.
text.NABRE: Blessed are you, for you have shown mercy toward two only children. Grant them, Master, mercy and protection, and bring their lives to fulfillment with happiness and mercy.”
text.NRSV: “Blessed are you because you had mercy on two only children. Grant them, O Master, mercy and safety, and bring their lives to fulfillment in gladness and mercy.”
text.DRB: & said: We bless thee Lord God of Israel, because it hath not chanced as we thought.

text.KJV: Then Raguel bade his seruants to fill the graue.
text.NABRE: Then Raguel told his servants to fill in the grave before dawn.
text.NRSV: Then he ordered his servants to fill in the grave before daybreak.
text.DRB: For thou hast done thy mercy with us, & hast excluded from us the enemy, that persecuted us.

text.KJV: And hee kept the wedding feast fourteene dayes.
text.NABRE: Wedding Feast. He asked his wife to bake many loaves of bread; he himself went out to the herd and brought two steers and four rams, which he ordered to be slaughtered. So they began to prepare the feast.
text.NRSV: After this he told his wife to bake many loaves of bread; and he went out to the herd and brought two steers and four rams and ordered them to be slaughtered. So they began to make preparations.
text.DRB: And thou hast taken pity upon two the only children. Make them Lord bless thee more fully, & to offer up to thee a sacrifice of thy praise, and of their health, that all nations may know, that thou art God only in all the earth.

text.KJV: For before the dayes of the mariage were finished, Raguel had said vnto him by an othe, that he should not depart, till the fourteene dayes of the mariage were expired,
text.NABRE: He summoned Tobiah and said to him, “For fourteen days you shall not stir from here, but shall remain here eating and drinking with me; you shall bring joy to my daughter’s afflicted spirit.
text.NRSV: Then he called Tobias and swore a solemn oath to him, “You shall not leave here for fourteen days, but shall stay here eating and drinking with me; and you shall bring joy to my daughter’s sorrowing spirit.”
text.DRB: And forthwith Raguel commanded his servants, that they should fill up the pit which they had made, before it were day.

text.KJV: And then he should take the halfe of his goods, and goe in safetie to his father, and should haue the rest when I and my wife be dead.
text.NABRE: Now take half of what I own here; go back in good health to your father. The other half will be yours when I and my wife die. Take courage, son! I am your father, and Edna is your mother; we belong to you and to your sister both now and forever. So take courage, son!”
text.NRSV: “Take one-half of all that I own and return in health to your father; the other half will be yours when I and my wife die. Take courage, my child. I am your father and Edna is your mother, and we belong to you as well as to your sister now and forever. Take courage, my child.”
text.DRB: And he bade his wife make ready a feast, and prepare all things, that for victuals were necessary to them that go a journey.
He caused also two fat kine, and four wethers to be killed, and great cheer to be prepared for all his neighbours and all his friends.
And Raguel adjured Tobias that he should abide with him two weeks.
And of all things which Raguel possessed, he gave the half part to Tobias, and made this writing, that the half part, which was remaining after their decease, should come to the dominion of Tobias.

text.KJV: Then Tobias called Raphael, and said vnto him,
text.NABRE: Chapter 9 The Money Recovered. Then Tobiah called Raphael and said to him:
text.NRSV: Then Tobias called Raphael and said to him,
text.DRB: Then Tobias called the Angel to him, whom he thought to be a man, and he said to him: Brother Azarias, I pray thee harken to my words:

text.KJV: Brother Azarias, Take with thee a seruant, and two camels, and go to Rages of Media to Gabael, & bring me the money, & bring him to the wedding.
text.NABRE: “Brother Azariah, take along with you from here four servants and two camels and travel to Rages. Go to Gabael’s house and give him this bond. Get the money and then bring him along with you to the wedding celebration.
text.NRSV: “Azariah, my brother, take four servants and two camels with you and travel to Rhages. Go to the house of Gabael and give him the bond, get the money, and then bring him with you to the wedding celebration.
text.DRB: If I should deliver myself to be thy servant I shall not deserve thy providence.

text.KJV: For Raguel hath sworne that I shall not depart.
text.NABRE: For you know that my father will be counting the days. If I should delay even by a single day, I would cause him intense grief.
text.NRSV: For you know that my father must be counting the days, and if I delay even one day I will cause him great distress.
text.DRB: Howbeit I beseech thee, that thou take unto thee beasts and servants, and go to Gabelus into Rages the city of Medes: and render him his hand writing, and receive of him the money, and desire him to come to my marriage.

text.KJV: But my father counteth the dayes, and if I tarie long, he will be very sorie.
text.NABRE: You have witnessed the oath that Raguel has sworn; I cannot violate his oath.”
text.NRSV: You see what Raguel has sworn, and I cannot break his oath.”
text.DRB: For thyself knowest that my father numbereth the days: and if I slack one day more, his soul is made sorrowful.

text.KJV: So Raphael went out and lodged with Gabael, and gaue him the handwriting, who brought forth bags, which were sealed vp, and gaue them to him.
text.NABRE: So Raphael, together with the four servants and two camels, traveled to Rages in Media, where they stayed at Gabael’s house. Raphael gave Gabael his bond and told him about Tobit’s son Tobiah, that he had married and was inviting him to the wedding celebration. Gabael got up and counted out for him the moneybags with their seals, and they packed them on the camels.
text.NRSV: So Raphael with the four servants and two camels went to Rhages in Media and stayed with Gabael. Raphael gave him the bond and informed him that Tobit’s son Tobias had married and was inviting him to the wedding celebration. So Gabael got up and counted out to him the money bags, with their seals intact; then they layered them on the camels.
text.DRB: And surely thou seest how Raguel hath adjured me, whose adjuring I cannot despise.

text.KJV: And earely in the morning they went forth both together, and came to the wedding, and Tobias blessed his wife.
text.NABRE: The following morning they both got an early start and traveled to the wedding celebration. When they entered Raguel’s house, they found Tobiah reclining at table. He jumped up and greeted Gabael, who wept and blessed him, exclaiming: “Good and noble child, son of a good and noble, righteous and charitable man, may the Lord bestow a heavenly blessing on you and on your wife, and on your wife’s father and mother. Blessed be God, because I have seen the very image of my cousin Tobit!”
text.NRSV: In the morning they both got up early and went to the wedding celebration. When they entered Raguel’s house, they found Tobias reclining at table. He sprang up and greeted Gabael, and Gabael wept and blessed him, saying, “Good and noble child, son of a good and noble, righteous and charitable man, may the Lord grant the blessing of heaven to you and your wife, and to your wife’s father and mother. Blessed be God, for I see in Tobias the very image of my cousin Tobit.”
text.DRB: Then Raphael taking four of Raguel's servants, and two camels, went into Rages the city of Medes: & finding Gabelus gave him his hand writing, and received of him all the money.
And he told him of Tobias the son of Tobias, all things that were done: & made him come with him to the marriage.
And when he was entered into Raguel's house, he found Tobias sitting at the table: and he leaping up, they kissed each other: and Gabelus wept, and blessed God,
and said: The God of Israel bless thee, because thou art the son of a very good man, and just, and that feareth God, and doth alms deeds:
and blessing be given upon thy wife, and upon your parents:
and that you may see your children, and your children's children, unto the third and fourth generation: and your seed be blessed of the God of Israel, who reigneth for ever and ever.
And when all had said, Amen, they went to the feast: but with the fear of our Lord also did they celebrate the feast of the marriage.

text.KJV: Nowe Tobit his father counted euery day, and when the dayes of the iourney were expired, and they came not:
text.NABRE: VI. Tobiah’s Return Journey to Nineveh and the Healing of Tobit Chapter 10 Anxiety of the Parents. Meanwhile, day by day, Tobit was keeping track of the time Tobiah would need to go and to return. When the number of days was reached and his son did not appear,
text.NRSV: Now day by day Tobit was keeping track of the days Tobias would need for going and for returning. When the days had passed and his son did not appear,
text.DRB: But when Tobias tarried long because of the marriage, Tobias his father was careful, saying: Why thinkest thou doth my son tarry, or why is he held there?

text.KJV: Then Tobit said, Are they detained? or is Gabael dead? and there is no man to giue him the money?
text.NABRE: he said, “Could it be that he has been detained there? Or perhaps Gabael has died, and there is no one to give him the money?”
text.NRSV: he said, “Is it possible that he has been detained? Or that Gabael has died, and there is no one to give him the money?”
text.DRB: Is Gabelus dead thinkest thou, and no man will restore him the money?

text.KJV: Therefore he was very sory.
text.NABRE: And he began to grieve.
text.NRSV: And he began to worry.
text.DRB: And he began to be sorrowful exceedingly himself, and Anna his wife with him: & they began both to weep together: because their son did not return to them the day appointed.

text.KJV: Then his wife said to him, My sonne is dead, seeing hee stayeth long, and she beganne to bewaile him, and said,
text.NABRE: His wife Anna said, “My son has perished and is no longer among the living!” And she began to weep aloud and to wail over her son:
text.NRSV: His wife Anna said, “My child has perished and is no longer among the living.” And she began to weep and lament for her son, saying,
text.DRB: His mother therefore wept with discomfortable tears, & said: Woe, woe is me, my son, why sent we thee to go to a strange country, the light of our eyes, the staff of our old age, the comfort of our life, the hope of our posterity?

text.KJV: Now I care for nothing, my sonne, since I haue let thee goe, the light of mine eyes.
text.NABRE: “Alas, child, light of my eyes, that I have let you make this journey!”
text.NRSV: “Woe to me, my child, the light of my eyes, that I let you make the journey.”
text.DRB: We having all things together in thee only, ought not to have let thee go from us.

text.KJV: To whom Tobit said, Hold thy peace, take no care; for he is safe.
text.NABRE: But Tobit kept telling her: “Be still, do not worry, my sister; he is safe! Probably they have to take care of some unexpected business there. The man who is traveling with him is trustworthy and one of our kindred. So do not grieve over him, my sister. He will be here soon.”
text.NRSV: But Tobit said to her, “Be quiet and stop worrying, my sister; he is in good health. They were probably delayed by some unforeseen event. The man who went with him is trustworthy and is one of our own kindred. Do not grieve for him, my sister; he will soon be here.”
text.DRB: To whom Tobias said: Hold thy peace, and be not troubled, our son is safe, that man with whom we sent him is faithful enough.

text.KJV: But she said, Hold thy peace, and deceiue me not: my sonne is dead, and she went out euery day into the way which they went, and did eate no meat on the day time, and ceased not whole nights, to bewaile her sonne Tobias, vntill the foureteene dayes of the wedding were expired, which Raguel had sworne, that he should spend there: Then Tobias said to Raguel, Let me goe, for my father, and my mother look no more to see me.
text.NABRE: But she retorted, “You be still, and do not try to deceive me! My son has perished!” She would rush out and keep watch every day at the road her son had taken. She ate nothing. After the sun had set, she would go back home to wail and cry the whole night through, getting no sleep at all. Departure from Ecbatana. Now when the fourteen days of the wedding celebration, which Raguel had sworn to hold for his daughter, had come to an end, Tobiah went to him and said: “Send me off, now, since I know that my father and mother do not believe they will ever see me again. So I beg you, father, let me depart and go back to my own father. I have already told you how I left him.”
text.NRSV: She retorted, “Be quiet yourself! Stop trying to deceive me! My child has perished.” She would rush out every day and watch the road her son had taken, and would eat nothing. When the sun had set she would go in and mourn and weep all night long, getting no sleep.
text.DRB: Howbeit she could by no means be comforted, but daily running out looked about, and went about all ways by which there seemed hope he would return, that she might see him a far off, if it were possible, coming.

text.KJV: But his father in law said vnto him, Tary with me, and I will send to thy father, and they shall declare vnto him, how things goe with thee.
text.NABRE: Raguel said to Tobiah: “Stay, son, stay with me. I am sending messengers to your father Tobit, and they will give him news of you.”
text.NRSV: When the fourteen days of the wedding celebration had ended that Raguel had sworn Tobias should spend there, Tobias came to him and said, “Send me back, for I know that my father and mother have given up hope of ever seeing me again. So I beg you, father, let me go, so that I may return to my own father; I have already explained to you how I left him.”
text.DRB: But Raguel said to his son in law: Tarry here, and I will send a messenger to Tobias thy father, that thou art in health.

text.KJV: But Tobias said, No: but let me goe to my father.
text.NABRE: But Tobiah insisted, “No, I beg you to send me back to my father.”
text.NRSV: But Raguel said to Tobias, “Stay, my child, stay with me; I will send messengers to your father Tobit and they will inform him of how you are.”
text.DRB: To whom Tobias said: I know that my father and my mother do now count the days, and their spirit is tormented in them.

text.KJV: Then Raguel arose and gaue him Sara his wife, and halfe his goods, seruants, & cattell, and money.
text.NABRE: Raguel then promptly handed over to Tobiah his wife Sarah, together with half of all his property: male and female slaves, oxen and sheep, donkeys and camels, clothing, money, and household goods.
text.NRSV: But Tobias said, “No! I beg you to send me back to my father.”
text.DRB: And when Raguel desired Tobias in many words, and he by no means would hear him, he delivered Sara unto him, & the half part of all his substance in men servants, & women servants, in cattle, in camels, and in kine, and in much money and dismist him safe and joyful from him,

text.KJV: And hee blessed them, and sent them away, saying, The God of heauen giue you a prosperous iourney, my children.
text.NABRE: He saw them safely off. Embracing Tobiah, he said to him: “Farewell, son. Have a safe journey. May the Lord of heaven grant prosperity to you and to your wife Sarah. And may I see children of yours before I die!”
text.NRSV: So Raguel promptly gave Tobias his wife Sarah, as well as half of all his property: male and female slaves, oxen and sheep, donkeys and camels, clothing, money, and household goods.
text.DRB: saying: The holy Angel of our Lord be in your journey, and bring you through safe, and that you may find all things well about your parents, and mine eyes may see your children before I die.

text.KJV: And he said to his daughter, Honour thy father and thy mother in law, which are now thy parents, that I may heare good report of thee: and hee kissed her. Edna also said to Tobias, The Lord of heauen restore thee, my deare brother, and grant that I may see thy children of my daughter Sara before I die, that I may reioyce before the Lord: behold, I commit my daughter vnto thee of speciall trust, wherefore doe not entreate her euill.
text.NABRE: Then he said to his daughter Sarah, “My daughter, honor your father-in-law and your mother-in-law, because from now on they are as much your parents as the ones who brought you into the world. Go in peace, daughter; let me hear a good report about you as long as I live.” Finally he said good-bye to them and let them go. Edna also said to Tobiah: “My child and beloved kinsman, may the Lord bring you back safely, and may I live long enough to see children of you and of my daughter Sarah before I die. Before the Lord, I entrust my daughter to your care. Never cause her grief all the days of your life. Go in peace, son. From now on I am your mother, and Sarah is your sister. Together may we all prosper throughout the days of our lives.” She kissed them both and saw them safely off.
text.NRSV: Then he saw them safely off, and he embraced Tobias and said, “Farewell, my child; have a safe journey. The Lord of heaven prosper you and your wife Sarah, and may I see children of yours before I die.”
text.DRB: And the parents taking their daughter, kissed her, and let her go:

text.NABRE: Tobiah left Raguel, full of happiness and joy, and he blessed the Lord of heaven and earth, the King of all, for making his journey so successful. Finally he blessed Raguel and his wife Edna, and added, “I have been commanded by the Lord to honor you all the days of your life!”
text.NRSV: Then he said to his daughter Sarah, “Go to your father-in-law’s house, for now they are as much your parents as those who gave you birth. Go in peace, my daughter; may I hear a good report of you as long as I live.” Then he kissed them both and saw them off.
text.DRB: admonishing her to honour her father and mother in law, to love her husband, to rule the household, to govern the house, and to shew herself irreprehensible.

text.rawText: Edna said to Tobias, “My child and beloved brother, may the Lord of heaven bring you back safely, and may I live long enough to see children of you and of my daughter Sarah before I die. In the sight of the Lord I entrust my daughter to you; do nothing to grieve her all the days of your life. Go in peace, my child. From now on I am your mother and Sarah is your sister. May we all prosper together all the days of our lives.” Then she kissed them both and saw them off in good health.

text.KJV: After these things Tobias went his way, praising God that he had giuen him a prosperous iourney, and blessed Raguel, and Edna his wife, and went on his way till they drew neere vnto Nineue.
text.NABRE: Chapter 11 Homeward Journey. As they drew near to Kaserin, which is opposite Nineveh,
text.NRSV: When they came near Kaserin, which is opposite Nineveh, Raphael said,
text.DRB: And when they returned they came to Charan, which is in the mid-way against Ninive, the eleventh day.

text.KJV: Then Raphael said to Tobias, Thou knowest brother, how thou didst leaue thy father.
text.NABRE: Raphael said: “You know how we left your father.
text.NRSV: “You know how we left your father.
text.DRB: And the Angel said: Brother Tobias thou knowest how thou didst leave thy father.

text.KJV: Let vs haste before thy wife, and prepare the house.
text.NABRE: Let us hurry on ahead of your wife to prepare the house while they are still on the way.”
text.NRSV: Let us hurry on ahead of your wife and prepare the house while they are still on the way.”
text.DRB: If it please thee therefore, let us go before, and let the families follow softly after us, together with thy wife, and with the beasts.

text.KJV: And take in thine hand the gall of the fish: so they went their way, and the dog went after them.
text.NABRE: So both went on ahead together, and Raphael said to him, “Take the gall in your hand!” And the dog ran along behind them.
text.NRSV: As they went on together, Raphael said, “Have the gall ready.” And the dog went along behind them.
text.DRB: And when this pleased him that they should go, Raphael said to Tobias: Take with thee of the gall of the fish: for it shall be necessary. Tobias therefore took of that gall and they departed.

text.KJV: Now Anna sate looking about towards the way for her sonne.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile, Anna sat watching the road by which her son was to come.
text.NRSV: Meanwhile Anna sat looking intently down the road for her son.
text.DRB: But Anna sat beside the way daily, in the top of a hill, from whence she might see a far off.

text.KJV: And when she espied him comming, she said to his father, Behold, thy sonne commeth, and the man that went with him.
text.NABRE: When she saw him coming, she called to his father, “Look, your son is coming, and the man who traveled with him!”
text.NRSV: She caught sight of him coming, and said to his father, “Look, your son is coming, and the man who went with him!”
text.DRB: And whilst she watched his coming out of that place, she saw a far off, and by and by perceived her son coming: and running she told her husband saying: Behold thy son cometh.

text.KJV: Then said Raphael, I know, Tobias, that thy father will open his eyes.
text.NABRE: Raphael said to Tobiah before he came near to his father: “I know that his eyes will be opened.
text.NRSV: Raphael said to Tobias, before he had reached his father, “I know that his eyes will be opened.
text.DRB: And Raphael said to Tobias: But when thou art entered into thy house forthwith adore our Lord thy God: and giving thanks to him go to thy father, and kiss him.

text.KJV: Therefore annoint thou his eies with the gall, and being pricked therewith he shall rub, and the whitenesse shall fall away, and he shall see thee.
text.NABRE: Apply the fish gall to his eyes, and the medicine will make the white scales shrink and peel off from his eyes; then your father will have sight again and will see the light of day.”
text.NRSV: Smear the gall of the fish on his eyes; the medicine will make the white films shrink and peel off from his eyes, and your father will regain his sight and see the light.”
text.DRB: And immediately anoint upon his eyes of this gall of the fish, which thou carriest with thee. For know thou that forthwith his eyes shall be opened, and thy father shall see the light of heaven, and shall rejoice in the sight of thee.

text.KJV: Then Anna ran forth, and fell vpon the necke of her sonne, and said vnto him, seeing I haue seene thee my sonne, from henceforth, I am content to die, and they wept both.
text.NABRE: Sight Restored. Then Anna ran up to her son, embraced him, and said to him, “Now that I have seen you again, son, I am ready to die!” And she sobbed aloud.
text.NRSV: Then Anna ran up to her son and threw her arms around him, saying, “Now that I have seen you, my child, I am ready to die.” And she wept.
text.DRB: Then ran the dog before, which had been with them in the way, and coming as it were a messenger with the fawning of his tail rejoiced.

text.KJV: Tobit also went forth toward the doore, and stumbled: but his sonne ran vnto him,
text.NABRE: Tobit got up and stumbled out through the courtyard gate to meet his son. Tobiah went up to him
text.NRSV: Then Tobit got up and came stumbling out through the courtyard door. Tobias went up to him,
text.DRB: And his father that was blind rising up began to run stumbling with his feet: and giving a servant his hand, went to meet his son.

text.KJV: And tooke hold of his father, and he strake of the gall on his fathers eyes, saying, Be of good hope, my father.
text.NABRE: with the fish gall in his hand and blew into his eyes. Holding him firmly, he said, “Courage, father.” Then he applied the medicine to his eyes, and it made them sting.
text.NRSV: with the gall of the fish in his hand, and holding him firmly, he blew into his eyes, saying, “Take courage, father.” With this he applied the medicine on his eyes,
text.DRB: And receiving him kissed him, with his wife, and they began both to weep for joy.

text.KJV: And when his eyes beganne to smart, he rubbed them.
text.NRSV: and it made them smart. Next, with both his hands he peeled off the white films from the corners of his eyes.
text.DRB: And when they had adored God, and given thanks, they sat down together.

text.KJV: And the whitenesse pilled away from the corners of his eyes, and when he saw his sonne, he fell vpon his necke.
text.NABRE: Tobiah used both hands to peel the white scales from the corners of his eyes. Tobit saw his son and threw his arms around him.
text.NRSV: Then Tobit threw his arms around him and wept,
text.DRB: Then Tobias taking of the gall of the fish, anointed his father's eyes.

text.KJV: And he wept, and said, Blessed art thou, O God, and blessed is thy Name for euer, and blessed are all thine holy Angels:
text.NABRE: Weeping, he exclaimed, “I can see you, son, the light of my eyes!” Then he prayed, “Blessed be God, blessed be his great name, and blessed be all his holy angels. May his great name be with us, and blessed be all the angels throughout all the ages.
text.NRSV: and he said, “I see you, my son, the light of my eyes!” Then he said, “Blessed be God, and blessed be his great name, and blessed be all his holy angels. May his holy name be blessed throughout all the ages,”
text.DRB: And he stayed as it were almost half an hour: and the white blemish began to come out of his eyes, as it were the skin of an egg.

text.KJV: For thou hast scourged, and hast taken pitie on me: for behold, I see my sonne Tobias. And his sonne went in reioycing, and told his father the great things that had happened to him in Media.
text.NABRE: God it was who afflicted me, and God who has had mercy on me. Now I see my son Tobiah!” Then Tobit went back in, rejoicing and praising God with full voice. Tobiah related to his father how his journey had been a success; that he had brought back the money; and that he had married Raguel’s daughter Sarah, who was about to arrive, for she was near the gate of Nineveh.
text.NRSV: “for it was he who scourged me, and it is he who has had mercy on me. Behold, I see my son Tobias!” Then Tobit went into his house rejoicing and blessing God with a loud voice. Tobias reported to his father that his journey had been successful, that he had brought the money, that he had married Sarah daughter of Raguel, and that she was, indeed, on her way, very near to the gate of Nineveh.
text.DRB: Which Tobias taking drew from his eyes, and immediately he received sight.

text.KJV: Then Tobit went out to meete his daughter in law at the gate of Niniue, reioycing and praysing God: and they which saw him goe, marueiled because he had receiued his sight.
text.NABRE: Rejoicing and blessing God, Tobit went out to the gate of Nineveh to meet his daughter-in-law. When the people of Nineveh saw him coming, walking along briskly, with no one leading him by the hand, they were amazed.
text.NRSV: Then Tobit, rejoicing and blessing God, went out to the gate of Nineveh to meet his daughter-in-law. When the people of Nineveh saw him coming, walking with a firm step and needing no one to lead him by the hand, they were amazed. Tobit testified before them that God had been merciful to him and had opened his eyes.
text.DRB: And they glorified God, to wit, himself and his wife, and all that knew him.

text.KJV: But Tobit gaue thankes before them: because God had mercy on him. And when hee came neere to Sara his daughter in Law, hee blessed her, saying, Thou art welcome daughter: God be blessed which hath brought thee vnto vs, and blessed be thy father and thy mother; And there was ioy amongst all his brethren which were at Nineue.
text.NABRE: Before them all Tobit proclaimed how God had shown mercy to him and opened his eyes. When Tobit came up to Sarah, the wife of his son Tobiah, he blessed her and said: “Welcome, my daughter! Blessed be your God for bringing you to us, daughter! Blessed are your father and your mother. Blessed be my son Tobiah, and blessed be you, daughter! Welcome to your home with blessing and joy. Come in, daughter!” That day there was joy for all the Jews who lived in Nineveh.
text.NRSV: When Tobit reached Sarah, the wife of his son Tobias, he blessed her, saying, “Welcome, my daughter! Blessed be God who has brought you to us, and blessed be your father and your mother.” So there was rejoicing among all his kindred in Nineveh.
text.DRB: And Tobias said: I bless thee Lord God of Israel, because thou hast chastised me, and thou hast saved me: and behold I see Tobias my son.

text.KJV: And Achiacharus, and Nasbas his brothers sonne came.
text.NABRE: Ahiqar and his nephew Nadin were also on hand to rejoice with Tobit. Tobiah’s wedding feast was celebrated with joy for seven days, and many gifts were given to him.
text.NRSV: Ahikar and his nephew Nadab were also there to share Tobit’s joy.
text.DRB: After seven days also came in Sara his son's wife, & all the family safe, & cattle, & the camels, and much money of his wife's: and that money also, which he had received of Gabelus:

text.KJV: And Tobias wedding was kept seuen dayes with great ioy.
text.NRSV: With merriment they celebrated Tobias’s wedding for seven days, and many gifts were given to him.
text.DRB: and he told his parents all the benefits of God, which he had done to him by the man that conducted him.
And Achior and Nabath Tobias' sister's sons came, rejoicing at Tobias, and congratulating him for all good things that God had shewed towards him.
And for seven days making good cheer, they rejoiced all with great joy.

text.KJV: Then Tobit called his son Tobias, and said vnto him, My sonne, see that the man haue his wages, which went with thee, and thou must giue him more.
text.NABRE: VII. Raphael Reveals His Identity Chapter 12 Raphael’s Wages. When the wedding celebration came to an end, Tobit called his son Tobiah and said to him, “Son, see to it that you pay his wages to the man who made the journey with you and give him a bonus too.”
text.NRSV: When the wedding celebration was ended, Tobit called his son Tobias and said to him, “My child, see to paying the wages of the man who went with you, and give him a bonus as well.”
text.DRB: Then Tobias called to him his son, and said to him: What may we give to this holy man, that is come with thee?

text.KJV: And Tobias said vnto him, O father, it is no harme to me to giue him halfe of those things which I haue brought.
text.NABRE: Tobiah said: “Father, how much shall I pay him? It would not hurt to give him half the wealth he brought back with me.
text.NRSV: He replied, “Father, how much shall I pay him? It would do no harm to give him half of the possessions I brought back with me.
text.DRB: Tobias answering, said to his father: Father what reward shall we give him? Or what can be worthy of his benefits?

text.KJV: For he hath brought me againe to thee in safety, and made whole my wife, and brought mee the money, and likewise healed thee.
text.NABRE: He led me back safe and sound, healed my wife, brought the money back with me, and healed you. How much should I pay him?”
text.NRSV: For he has led me back to you safely, he cured my wife, he brought the money back with me, and he healed you.”
text.DRB: He hath led me & brought me again safe, he received the money of Gabelus, he caused me to have my wife, and the evil spirit he chased from her, he made joy unto her parents, myself he delivered from being devoured of the fish, thee also he hath made to see the light of heaven, and we are replenished with all good things by him. What can we give him worthy for these things?

text.KJV: Then the old man said: It is due vnto him.
text.NABRE: Tobit answered, “It is only fair, son, that he should receive half of all that he brought back.”
text.NRSV: The old man said, “He is certainly entitled to it.”
text.DRB: But I beseech thee my father, that thou desire him, if perhaps he will vouchsafe to take unto him the one half of all things which are brought.

text.KJV: So he called the Angell, and he said vnto him, Take halfe of all that yee haue brought, and goe away in safety.
text.NABRE: So Tobiah called Raphael and said, “Take as your wages half of all that you have brought back, and farewell!” Exhortation.
text.NRSV: So Tobias called the angel and said to him, “Take half of all that you brought back, as your wages, and go in peace.”
text.DRB: And they calling him, to wit the father and the son, took him aside: and began to desire him that he would vouchsafe to accept the half part of all things, that they had brought.

text.KJV: Then he tooke them both apart, and sayd vnto them, Blesse God, praise him, and magnifie him, and praise him for the things which he hath done vnto you in the sight of all that liue. It is good to praise God and exalt his name, & honorably to shew forth the works of God, therfore be not slacke to praise him.
text.NABRE: Raphael called the two of them aside privately and said to them: “Bless God and give him thanks before all the living for the good things he has done for you, by blessing and extolling his name in song. Proclaim before all with due honor the deeds of God, and do not be slack in thanking him.
text.NRSV: Then Raphael called the two of them privately and said to them, “Bless God and acknowledge him in the presence of all the living for the good things he has done for you. Bless and sing praises to his name. With fitting honor declare to all people the deeds of God. Do not be slow to acknowledge him.”
text.DRB: Then he said to them secretly: Bless ye the God of heaven, and before all that live confess to him, because he hath done mercy with you.

text.KJV: It is good to keepe close the secret of a King, but it is honorable to reueale the works of God: do that which is good, and no euill shall touch you.
text.NABRE: A king’s secret should be kept secret, but one must declare the works of God and give thanks with due honor. Do good, and evil will not overtake you.
text.NRSV: “It is good to guard the secret of a king, but gloriously to reveal the works of God. Do good and evil will not overtake you.”
text.DRB: For to hide the secret of a king is good: but to reveal and confess the works of God is an honourable thing.

text.KJV: Praier is good with fasting, and almes and righteousnesse: a little with righteousnes is better then much with vnrighteousnesse: it is better to giue almes then to lay vp gold.
text.NABRE: Prayer with fasting is good. Almsgiving with righteousness is better than wealth with wickedness. It is better to give alms than to store up gold,
text.NRSV: “Prayer with fasting is good, but better than both is almsgiving with righteousness. A little with righteousness is better than wealth with wrongdoing. It is better to give alms than to lay up gold.”
text.DRB: Prayer is good with fasting and alms, rather than to lay up treasures of gold:

text.KJV: For almes doth deliuer from death, and shall purge away all sinne. Those that exercise almes, and righteousnesse, shall be filled with life.
text.NABRE: for almsgiving saves from death, and purges all sin. Those who give alms will enjoy a full life,
text.NRSV: “For almsgiving saves from death and purges away every sin. Those who give alms will enjoy a full life,”
text.DRB: because alms delivereth from death, and that is it which purgeth sins, and maketh to find mercy and life everlasting.

text.KJV: But they that sinne are enemies to their owne life.
text.NABRE: but those who commit sin and do evil are their own worst enemies.
text.NRSV: “but those who commit sin and do wrong are their own worst enemies.”
text.DRB: But they that commit sin and iniquity, are enemies to their own soul.

text.KJV: Surely I will keep close nothing from you. For I said, it was good to keepe close the secret of a King, but that it was honorable to reueale the works of God.
text.NABRE: Raphael’s Identity. “I shall now tell you the whole truth and conceal nothing at all from you. I have already said to you, ‘A king’s secret should be kept secret, but one must declare the works of God with due honor.’
text.NRSV: “I will now declare the whole truth to you and will conceal nothing from you. I have already declared it to you when I said, ‘It is good to guard the secret of a king, but gloriously to reveal the works of God.’”
text.DRB: I open therefore unto you the truth, and I will not hide from you the secret word.

text.KJV: Now therefore, when thou didst pray, and Sara thy daughter in Law, I did bring the remembrance of your prayers before the holy one, and when thou didst bury the dead, I was with thee likewise.
text.NABRE: Now when you, Tobit, and Sarah prayed, it was I who presented the record of your prayer before the Glory of the Lord; and likewise whenever you used to bury the dead.
text.NRSV: “So now when you and Sarah prayed, it was I who brought and read the record of your prayer before the glory of the Lord, and likewise whenever you would bury the dead.”
text.DRB: When thou didst pray with tears, and didst bury the dead, and left thy dinner, and didest hide the dead by day in thy house, and by night didst bury them, I offered thy prayer to our Lord.

text.KJV: And when thou didst not delay to rise vp, and leaue thy dinner to go and couer the dead, thy good deede was not hidde from me: but I was with thee.
text.NABRE: When you did not hesitate to get up and leave your dinner in order to go and bury that dead man,
text.NRSV: “And that time when you did not hesitate to get up and leave your dinner to go and bury the dead, I was sent to test you.”
text.DRB: And because thou wast acceptable to God, it was necessary that tentation should prove thee.

text.KJV: And now God hath sent mee to heale thee, & Sara thy daughter in law.
text.NABRE: I was sent to put you to the test. At the same time, however, God sent me to heal you and your daughter-in-law Sarah.
text.NRSV: “And at the same time God sent me to heal you and Sarah your daughter-in-law.”
text.DRB: And now our Lord sent me to cure thee, and to deliver Sara thy son's wife from the devil.

text.KJV: I am Raphael one of the seuen holy Angels, which present the prayers of the Saints, and which go in and out before the glory of the Holy one.
text.NABRE: I am Raphael, one of the seven angels who stand and serve before the Glory of the Lord.”
text.NRSV: “I am Raphael, one of the seven angels who stand ready and enter before the glory of the Lord.”
text.DRB: For I am Raphael an Angel, one of the seven, which assist before our Lord.

text.KJV: Then they were both troubled, and fel vpon their faces: for they feared.
text.NABRE: Greatly shaken, the two of them fell prostrate in fear.
text.NRSV: The two of them were shaken; they fell on their faces, for they were afraid.
text.DRB: And when they had heard these things, they were troubled, and trembling fell upon the ground on their face.

text.KJV: But he said vnto them, feare not, for it shall go well with you, praise God therefore.
text.NABRE: But Raphael said to them: “Do not fear; peace be with you! Bless God now and forever.
text.NRSV: But he said to them, “Do not be afraid; peace be with you. Bless God forever.”
text.DRB: And the Angel said to them: Peace be to you, fear not.

text.KJV: For not of any fauour of mine, but by the will of our God I came, wherefore praise him for euer.
text.NABRE: As for me, when I was with you, I was not acting out of any favor on my part, but by God’s will. So bless God every day; give praise with song.
text.NRSV: “As for me, when I was with you, I was not there by any favor of mine, but by the will of God. Bless him all your days; sing praises to him.”
text.DRB: For when I was with you, by the will of God I was so: bless ye him, and sing to him.

text.KJV: All these daies I did appeare vnto you, but I did neither eat nor drinke, but you did see a vision.
text.NABRE: Even though you saw me eat and drink, I did not eat or drink anything; what you were seeing was a vision.
text.NRSV: “When you were looking at me, I was not eating anything; I only appeared to be doing so.”
text.DRB: Iseemed indeed to eat with you, and to drink: but I use an invisible meat and drink, which cannot be seen of men.

text.KJV: Now therefore giue God thanks: for I go vp to him þ sent me, but write all things which are done, in a booke.
text.NABRE: So now bless the Lord on earth and give thanks to God. Look, I am ascending to the one who sent me. Write down all that has happened to you.” And he ascended.
text.NRSV: “So now get up from the ground, and acknowledge God. See, I am ascending to him who sent me. Write down all these things that have happened to you.” And he ascended.
text.DRB: It is time therefore that I return to him, that sent me: but bless ye God, and tell all his marvelous works.

text.KJV: And when they rose, they saw him no more.
text.NABRE: They stood up but were no longer able to see him.
text.NRSV: Then they stood up and could no longer see him.
text.DRB: And when he had said these things, he was taken from their sight, and they could see him no more.

text.KJV: Then they confessed the great and wonderfull workes of God, and how the Angel of the Lord had appeared vnto them.
text.NABRE: They kept blessing God and singing his praises, and they continued to give thanks for these marvelous works that God had done, because an angel of God appeared to them.
text.NRSV: They kept blessing God and singing his praises, and they acknowledged God for these marvelous deeds of his, when an angel of God had appeared to them.
text.DRB: Then prostrate for three hours upon their face, they blessed God: and rising up they told all his marvelous works.

text.KJV: Then Tobit wrote a prayer of reioycing, and said, Blessed be God that liueth for euer, and blessed be his kingdome:
text.NABRE: VIII. Tobit’s Song of Praise Chapter 13 Then Tobit spoke and composed a song of joyful praise; he said: Blessed be God who lives forever, because his kingship lasts for all ages.
text.NRSV: Then Tobit said: “Blessed be God who lives forever, because his kingdom lasts throughout all ages.”
text.DRB: And Tobias the elder opening his mouth, blessed our Lord, and said: Thou art great Lord for ever, and thy Kingdom world without end:

text.KJV: For he doeth scourge, and hath mercy: hee leadeth downe to hell, and bringeth vp againe: neither is there any that can auoid his hand.
text.NABRE: For he afflicts and shows mercy, casts down to the depths of Hades, brings up from the great abyss. What is there that can snatch from his hand?
text.NRSV: “For he scourges and has mercy; he leads down to Hades in the lowest regions of the earth, and he brings up from the great abyss, and there is nothing that can escape his hand.”
text.DRB: because thou scourgest, and savest: leadest down to hell, and bringest back again: and there is none that may escape thy hand.

text.KJV: Confesse him before the Gentiles, ye children of Israel: for he hath scattered vs among them.
text.NABRE: Give thanks to him, you Israelites, in the presence of the nations, for though he has scattered you among them,
text.NRSV: “Acknowledge him before the nations, O children of Israel; for he has scattered you among them.”
text.DRB: Confess to our Lord ye children of Israel, and in the sight of the Gentiles praise him:

text.KJV: There declare his greatnesse, and extoll him before all the liuing, for he is our Lord, and he is the God our father for euer:
text.NABRE: even there recount his greatness. Exalt him before every living being, because he is your Lord, and he is your God, our Father and God forever and ever!
text.NRSV: “He has shown you his greatness even there. Exalt him in the presence of every living being, because he is our Lord and he is our God; he is our Father and he is God forever.”
text.DRB: because he hath therefore dispersed you among the gentiles, which know not him, that you may declare his marvelous works, and make them know, that there is no other God omnipotent besides him.

text.KJV: And he wil scourge vs for our iniquities, and will haue mercy againe, and will gather vs out of all nations, among whom he hath scattered vs.
text.NABRE: He will afflict you for your iniquities, but will have mercy on all of you. He will gather you from all the nations among whom you have been scattered.
text.NRSV: “He will scourge you for your iniquities, but he will again have mercy on all of you. He will gather you from all the nations among whom you have been scattered.”
text.DRB: He hath chastised us for our iniquities: and he will save us for his mercy.

text.KJV: If you turne to him with your whole heart, and with your whole minde, and deale vprightly before him, then will hee turne vnto you, and will not hide his face from you: Therefore see what he will doe with you, and confesse him with your whole mouth, and praise the Lord of might, and extoll the euerlasting King: in the land of my captiuitie doe I praise him, and declare his might and maiesty to a sinnefull nation: O yee sinners turne, and doe iustice before him: who can tell if he will accept you, and haue mercy on you?
text.NABRE: When you turn back to him with all your heart, and with all your soul do what is right before him, Then he will turn to you, and will hide his face from you no longer. Now consider what he has done for you, and give thanks with full voice. Bless the Lord of righteousness, and exalt the King of the ages. In the land of my captivity I give thanks, and declare his power and majesty to a sinful nation. According to your heart do what is right before him: perhaps there will be pardon for you.
text.NRSV: “If you turn to him with all your heart and with all your soul, to do what is true before him, then he will turn to you and will no longer hide his face from you. So now see what he has done for you; acknowledge him at the top of your voice. Bless the Lord of righteousness, and exalt the King of the ages. In the land of my exile I acknowledge him, and show his power and majesty to a nation of sinners: ‘Turn back, you sinners, and do what is right before him; perhaps he may look favorably upon you and show you mercy.’”
text.DRB: Behold therefore what he hath done with us, and with fear & trembling confess ye to him: and extol the King of the worlds in your works.

text.KJV: I wil extoll my God, and my soule shal praise the King of heauen, and shal reioyce in his greatnesse.
text.NABRE: As for me, I exalt my God, my soul exalts the King of heaven, and rejoices all the days of my life. Let all sing praise to his greatness,
text.NRSV: “As for me, I exalt my God, and my spirit rejoices in the King of heaven.”
text.DRB: And I in the land of my captivity will confess to him: because he hath shewed his majesty toward a sinful nation.

text.KJV: Let all men speake, and let all praise him for his righteousnesse.
text.NABRE: let all speak and give thanks in Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: “Let all people speak of his majesty, and acknowledge him in Jerusalem.”
text.DRB: Convert therefore ye sinners, and do justice before God, believing that he will do his mercy with you.

text.KJV: O Ierusalem the holy Citie, he will scourge thee for thy childrens workes, and will haue mercy againe on the sonnes of the righteous.
text.NABRE: Jerusalem, holy city, he will afflict you for the works of your hands, but will again pity the children of the righteous.
text.NRSV: “O Jerusalem, the holy city, he scourged you for the works of your hands, but will again have mercy on the children of the righteous.”
text.DRB: And I and my soul will rejoice in him.

text.KJV: Giue praise to the Lord, for hee is good: and praise the euerlasting King, that his Tabernacle may bee builded in thee againe with ioy: and let him make ioyfull there in thee, those that are captiues, and loue in thee for euer those that are miserable.
text.NABRE: Give thanks to the Lord with righteousness, and bless the King of the ages, so that your tabernacle may be rebuilt in you with joy. May he gladden within you all who are captives; may he cherish within you all who are distressed for all generations to come.
text.NRSV: “Acknowledge the Lord worthily, and bless the King of the ages, so that his tent may be rebuilt in you in joy. May he cheer all those within you who are captives, and love all those within you who are distressed, to all generations forever.”
text.DRB: Bless ye our Lord all his elect, celebrate days of gladness, and confess to him.

text.KJV: Many nations shall come from farre to the Name of the Lord God, with gifts in their hands, euen giftes to the King of heauen: all generations shall praise thee with great ioy.
text.NABRE: A bright light will shine to the limits of the earth. Many nations will come to you from afar, And inhabitants of all the ends of the earth to your holy name, Bearing in their hands gifts for the King of heaven. Generation after generation will offer joyful worship in you; your name will be great forever and ever.
text.NRSV: “A bright light will shine to all the ends of the earth; many nations from far away will come to you, and the inhabitants of the remotest parts of the earth to your holy name, bearing gifts in their hands for the King of heaven. Generation after generation will give joyful praise in you; the name of the chosen city will endure forever.”
text.DRB: Jerusalem the city of God, our Lord hath chastised thee in the works of thy hands.

text.KJV: Cursed are all they which hate thee, and blessed shall all be, which loue thee for euer.
text.NABRE: Cursed be all who despise you and revile you; cursed be all who hate you and speak a harsh word against you; cursed be all who destroy you and pull down your walls, And all who overthrow your towers and set fire to your homes. But blessed forever be all those who respect you.
text.NRSV: “Cursed be all who speak a harsh word against you; cursed be all who conquer you and pull down your walls, all who overthrow your towers and set your homes on fire. But blessed forever will be all who revere you.”
text.DRB: Confess to our Lord in thy good things, and bless the God of the worlds, that he may re-edify his tabernacle in thee, and may call back all the captives to thee, and thou mayst rejoice for ever and ever.

text.KJV: Reioyce & be glad for the children of the iust: for they shall be gathered together, & shall blesse the Lord of the iust.
text.NABRE: Go, then, rejoice and exult over the children of the righteous, for they will all be gathered together and will bless the Lord of the ages.
text.NRSV: “Go, then, and rejoice over the children of the righteous, for they will all be gathered together and will bless the Lord of the ages.”
text.DRB: Thou shalt shine with a glorious light: and all the coasts of the earth shall adore thee.

text.KJV: O blessed are they which loue thee, for they shall reioyce in thy peace: blessed are they which haue been sorowfull for all thy scourges, for they shal reioyce for thee, when they haue seene all thy glory, and shalbe glad for euer.
text.NABRE: Happy are those who love you, and happy are those who rejoice in your peace. Happy too are all who grieve over all your afflictions, For they will rejoice over you and behold all your joy forever.
text.NRSV: “Happy are those who love you, and happy are those who rejoice in your prosperity. Happy also are all people who grieve with you because of all your scourges; for they will rejoice with you and witness all your glory forever.”
text.DRB: Nations from far shall come to thee: and bringing gifts, they shall adore our Lord in thee, and shall esteem thy land for sanctification.

text.KJV: Let my soule blesse God the great King.
text.NABRE: My soul, bless the Lord, the great King;
text.NRSV: “My spirit blesses the Lord, the great King.”
text.DRB: For they shall invocate the great name in thee.

text.KJV: For Ierusalem shall be built vp with Saphires, and Emerauds, and precious stone: thy walles and towres, and battlements with pure golde.
text.NABRE: for Jerusalem will be rebuilt as his house forever. Happy too will I be if a remnant of my offspring survives to see your glory and to give thanks to the King of heaven! The gates of Jerusalem will be built with sapphire and emerald, and all your walls with precious stones. The towers of Jerusalem will be built with gold, and their battlements with purest gold.
text.NRSV: “For Jerusalem will be built as his house for all ages. How happy I will be if a remnant of my descendants should survive to see your glory and acknowledge the King of heaven. The gates of Jerusalem will be built with sapphire and emerald, and all your walls with precious stones. The towers of Jerusalem will be built with gold, and their battlements with pure gold. The streets of Jerusalem will be paved with ruby and with stones of Ophir.”
text.DRB: Cursed shall they be that shall contemn thee: and damned shall they be that shall blaspheme thee: and blessed shall they be that shall build thee.

text.KJV: And the streets of Ierusalem shal be paued with Berill, and Carbuncle, and stones of Ophir.
text.NABRE: The streets of Jerusalem will be paved with rubies and stones of Ophir;
text.NRSV: “The gates of Jerusalem will sing hymns of joy, and all her houses will say, ‘Hallelujah! Blessed be the God of Israel!’ and the blessed will bless the holy name forever and ever.”
text.DRB: And thou shalt rejoice in thy children, because they shall all be blessed, and shall be gathered together to our Lord.
Blessed are all that love thee, and that rejoice upon thy peace.
text.NABRE: The gates of Jerusalem will sing hymns of gladness, and all its houses will cry out, Hallelujah! Blessed be the God of Israel for all ages! For in you the blessed will bless the holy name forever and ever.
text.KJV: And all her streets shall say, Halleluiah, and they shall praise him, saying, Blessed be God which hath extolled it for euer.

text.DRB: My soul, bless thou our Lord, because he hath delivered Jerusalem his city from all her tribulations, the Lord our God.
Blessed shall I be if there shall remain of my seed, to see the glory of Jerusalem
The gates of Jerusalem shall be built of Sapphire and the Emerald: and all the compass of the walls thereof of precious stone.
With white and clean stone shall all the streets thereof be paved: and in the streets thereof Alleluia shall be sung.
Blessed be our Lord, which hath exalted it, and his Kingdom be for ever and ever over it. Amen.

text.KJV: So Tobit made an ende of praising God.
text.NABRE: IX. Epilogue Chapter 14 Parting Advice. So the words of Tobit’s hymn of praise came to an end. Tobit died in peace at the age of a hundred and twelve and was buried with honor in Nineveh.
text.NRSV: So ended Tobit’s words of praise.
text.DRB: And the words of Tobias were ended. And after that Tobias was restored to his sight, he lived two and forty years, and saw the children of his nephews.

text.KJV: And he was eight and fifty yeeres olde when hee lost his sight, which was restored to him after eight yeeres, and he gaue almes, and he increased in the feare of the Lord God, and praised him.
text.NABRE: He was fifty-eight years old when he lost his eyesight, and after he recovered it he lived in prosperity, giving alms; he continued to fear God and give thanks to the divine Majesty.
text.NRSV: Tobit died in peace when he was one hundred twelve years old, and was buried with great honor in Nineveh. He was sixty-two years old when he lost his eyesight, and after regaining it he lived in prosperity, giving alms and continually blessing God and acknowledging God’s majesty.
text.DRB: Therefore an hundred and two years being accomplished, he was buried honourably in Ninive.

text.KJV: And when he was very aged, hee called his sonne, and the sixe sons of his sonne, and said to him, My sonne, take thy children; for behold, I am aged, and am ready to depart out of this life.
text.NABRE: As he was dying, he summoned his son Tobiah and Tobiah’s seven sons, and commanded him, “Son, take your children
text.NRSV: When he was about to die, he called his son Tobias and the seven sons of Tobias and gave this command: “My son, take your children
text.DRB: For being six and fifty years old he lost the sight of his eyes, and being threescore he received it again.

text.KJV: Goe into Media, my sonne, for I surely beleeue those things which Ionas the Prophet spake of Nineue, that it shall be ouerthrowen, and that for a time peace shal rather be in Media, and that our brethren shall lie scattered in the earth from that good land, and Ierusalem shall be desolate, and the house of God in it shalbe burned, and shall be desolate for a time:
text.NABRE: and flee into Media, for I believe God’s word that Nahum spoke against Nineveh. It will all happen and will overtake Assyria and Nineveh; indeed all that was said by Israel’s prophets whom God sent will come to pass. Not one of all their words will remain unfulfilled, but everything will take place in the time appointed for it. So it will be safer in Media than in Assyria or Babylon. For I know and believe that whatever God has said will be accomplished. It will happen, and not a single word of the prophecies will fail. As for our kindred who dwell in the land of Israel, they will all be scattered and taken into captivity from the good land. All the land of Israel will become a wilderness; even Samaria and Jerusalem will be a wilderness! For a time, the house of God will be desolate and will be burned.
text.NRSV: and hurry off to Media, for I believe the word of God that Nahum spoke about Nineveh, that all these things will take place and overtake Assyria and Nineveh. Indeed, everything that was spoken by the prophets of Israel, whom God sent, will occur. None of their words will fail, but all will take place at their appointed times. So it will be safer in Media than in Assyria and Babylon. For I know and believe that whatever God has said will be fulfilled and will come true; not a single word of the prophecies will fail. All of our kindred, inhabitants of the land of Israel, will be scattered and taken as captives from the good land; and the whole land of Israel will be desolate, even Samaria and Jerusalem will be desolate. And the temple of God in it will be burned to the ground, and it will be desolate for a while.”
text.DRB: And the rest of his life was in joy, and with great increase of the fear of God he went forward in peace.

text.KJV: And that againe God will haue mercie on them, and bring them againe into the land where they shall build a Temple, but not like to the first, vntill the time of that age be fulfilled, and afterward they shall returne from all places of their captiuitie, and build vp Ierusalem gloriously, and the house of God shall be built in it for euer, with a glorious building, as the prophets haue spoken thereof.
text.NABRE: But God will again have mercy on them and bring them back to the land of Israel. They will build the house again, but it will not be like the first until the era when the appointed times will be completed. Afterward all of them will return from their captivity, and they will rebuild Jerusalem with due honor. In it the house of God will also be rebuilt, just as the prophets of Israel said of it.
text.NRSV: “But God will again have mercy on them, and God will bring them back into the land of Israel; and they will rebuild the temple of God, but not like the first one until the period when the times of the ages are completed. Thereafter they will all return from their exile and will rebuild Jerusalem in splendor; and in it the temple of God will be rebuilt, just as the prophets of Israel have said concerning it.”
text.DRB: And at the hour of his death he called unto him Tobias his son, and his seven young sons, his nephews, and said to them.
The destruction of Ninive is near: for the word of our Lord faileth not: and our brethren, which are dispersed from the land of Israel, shall return to it.
text.NABRE: All the nations of the world will turn and reverence God in truth; all will cast away their idols, which have deceitfully led them into error.
text.KJV: And all nations shall turne, and feare the Lord God truely, and shall burie their idoles.

text.DRB: And all the desert land thereof shall be replenished, & the house of God which is burnt in it, shall again be re-edified: and thither shall all return that fear God,
text.NABRE: They will bless the God of the ages in righteousness. All the Israelites truly mindful of God, who are to be saved in those days, will be gathered together and will come to Jerusalem; in security will they dwell forever in the land of Abraham, which will be given to them. Those who love God sincerely will rejoice, but those who commit sin and wickedness will disappear completely from the land.
text.KJV: So shall all nations praise the Lord, and his people shal confesse God, and the Lord shall exalt his people, and all those which loue the Lord God in trueth and iustice, shall reioyce, shewing mercie to our brethren.

text.DRB: and the Gentiles shall forsake their idols, and shall come into Jerusalem; and shall inhabit in it,
text.KJV: And now, my sonne, depart out of Nineue, because that those things which the Prophet Ionas spake, shall surely come to passe.

text.DRB: and all the kings of the earth shall rejoice in it, adoring the king of Israel.
text.NABRE: “Now, my children, I give you this command: serve God sincerely and do what is pleasing in his sight; you must instruct your children to do what is right and to give alms, to be mindful of God and at all times to bless his name sincerely and with all their strength. Now, as for you, son, leave Nineveh; do not stay here.
text.KJV: But keepe thou the Law and the Commandements, and shew thy selfe mercifull and iust, that it may goe well with thee.

text.DRB: Hear ye therefore my children your father: serve our Lord in truth, and seek to do the things that please him:
text.NABRE: The day you bury your mother next to me, do not even stay overnight within the confines of the city. For I see that there is much wickedness in it, and much treachery is practiced in it, and people are not ashamed. See, my son, all that Nadin did to Ahiqar, the very one who reared him. Was not Ahiqar brought down alive into the earth? Yet God made Nadin’s disgraceful crime rebound against him. Ahiqar came out again into the light, but Nadin went into the everlasting darkness, for he had tried to kill Ahiqar. Because Ahiqar had given alms he escaped from the deadly trap Nadin had set for him. But Nadin fell into the deadly trap himself, and it destroyed him.
text.KJV: And burie me decently, and thy mother with me, but tarie no longer at Nineue. Remember, my sonne, how Aman handled Achiacharus þ brought him vp, how out of light he brought him into darkenes, and how he rewarded him againe: yet Ahiacharus was saued, but the other had his reward, for hee went downe into darkenesse. Manasses gaue almes, and escaped the snares of death which they had set for him: but Aman fell into the snare and perished.

text.DRB: and command your children that they do justices and alms deeds, that they be mindful of God, and bless him at all time in truth, and in all their power.
text.NABRE: So, my children, see what almsgiving does, and also what wickedness does—it kills! But now my spirit is about to leave me.” Death of Tobit and Tobiah. They laid him on his bed, and he died; and he was buried with honor.
text.KJV: Wherefore now, my sonne, consider what almes doeth, and how righteousnesse doth deliuer. When he had said these things, he gaue vp the ghost in the bed, being an hundred, and eight and fiftie yeeres old, and he buried him honourably.
And when Anna his mother was dead, he buried her with his father: but Tobias departed with his wife and children to Ecbatane, to Raguel his father in law:
text.NABRE: When Tobiah’s mother died, he buried her next to his father. He then departed with his wife and children for Media, where he settled in Ecbatana with his father-in-law Raguel.
text.NRSV: “And so, my son, see what shiftlessness does, and what righteousness does—how righteousness delivers and shiftlessness kills. But now my spirit fails me.” Then they laid him on his bed, and he died; and he was buried with great honor.
text.DRB: Now therefore children hear me, and do not tarry here: but what day soever you shall bury your mother by me in one sepulchre, from thenceforth direct your steps to depart hence:

text.KJV: Where hee became old with honour, and hee buried his father and mother in lawe honourably, and hee inherited their substance, and his father Tobits.
text.NABRE: He took respectful care of his aging father-in-law and mother-in-law; and he buried them at Ecbatana in Media. Then he inherited Raguel’s estate as well as that of his father Tobit.
text.NRSV: When his mother died, Tobias buried her beside his father. Then he and his wife and his children returned to Media and settled in Ecbatana with Raguel his father-in-law.
text.DRB: for I see that the iniquity thereof will give it an end.

text.KJV: And he died at Ecbatane in Media, being an hundred and seuen and twentie yeeres old.
text.NABRE: He died highly respected at the age of one hundred seventeen.
text.NRSV: He treated his in-laws with great respect in their old age, and buried them in Ecbatana of Media. He inherited both the property of Raguel and that of his father Tobit.
text.DRB: And it came to pass after the death of his mother, Tobias departed out of Ninive with his wife, and children, and children's children, and returned to his father and mother in law.

text.KJV: But before he died, he heard of the destruction of Nineue, which was taken by Nabuchodonosor & Assuerus: and before his death hee reioyced ouer Nineue.
text.NABRE: But before he died, he saw and heard of the destruction of Nineveh. He saw the inhabitants of the city being led captive into Media by Cyaxares, the king of Media. Tobiah blessed God for all that he had done against the Ninevites and Assyrians. Before dying he rejoiced over Nineveh, and he blessed the Lord God forever and ever.
text.NRSV: He died in Ecbatana of Media at the age of one hundred seventeen years.
text.DRB: And he found them in health in good old age: and he took care of them, and he closed their eyes: and all the inheritance of Raguel's house he received: and he saw the fifth generation, his children's children.
And ninety nine years being accomplished in the fear of our Lord, with joy they buried him.
And all his kindred, and all his generation continued in good life, and in holy conversation, so that they were acceptable both to God, and to men, and to all the inhabitants in the land.
